summary: Global equities are in their fourth consecutive week of selling pressure, driven by the ongoing Iran-U.S. conflict now in week four with no diplomatic resolution. Market expectations around the Fed's next move have shifted from a potential cut to a potential hike, driven by higher-for-longer oil price expectations tied to the Hormuz crisis.
tweet: Hormuz still closed, rate-hike odds now at 24% for June — and a fresh wave of Section 301 trade probes keeps multi-asset fear entrenched heading into the week's close.
